content.problemStatement:
You may have tried your level best to help Chef but Dr Doof has managed to come up with his masterplan in the meantime. Sadly, you have to help Chef once again. Dr Doof has designed a parenthesis-inator. It throws a stream of NNN brackets at the target, 111 bracket per second. The brackets can either be opening or closing. Chef appears in front of the stream at time ttt. If Chef faces an opening bracket, he gets hit. However, if he faces a closing bracket, he may choose to let it pass through him (Chef is immune to closing brackets). Chef gets a chance to counter attack Doof as soon as he finds a balanced non-empty bracket sequence. Help Chef by providing him the minimum time xxx at which he will be able to launch his counter attack. If Chef is unable to counter attack, answer ‚àí1-1‚àí1.

Formally, you are given a string SSS of length NNN consisting only of opening brackets ((( and closing brackets ))). The substring of SSS starting at index LLL and ending at index RRR, i.e. SLSL+1‚Ä¶SRS_L S_{L+1} \ldots S_{R}SL‚ÄãSL+1‚Äã‚Ä¶SR‚Äã is denoted by S[L,R]S[L, R]S[L,R] . Consider QQQ cases. In the ithi^{\text{th}}ith case, Chef appears at time tit_iti‚Äã (1‚â§ti‚â§N)(1 \leq t_i \leq N)(1‚â§ti‚Äã‚â§N) and faces all characters from index tit_iti‚Äã to NNN. Find the minimum index xxx (ti‚â§x‚â§N)(t_i \leq x \leq N)(ti‚Äã‚â§x‚â§N) such that the substring S[ti,x]S[t_i, x]S[ti‚Äã,x] contains a **non-empty balanced** bracket subsequence containing the same number of opening brackets as S[ti,x]S[t_i, x]S[ti‚Äã,x] (i.e., you cannot remove any opening bracket from the substring). If such an xxx does not exist, print ‚àí1-1‚àí1.

A string XXX is called a subsequence of a string YYY if it is possible to obtain XXX by erasing some (possibly zero) characters from YYY without changing the order of the remaining characters.

A balanced bracket sequence is defined as:


an empty string is a balanced bracket sequence.
if sss is a balanced bracket sequence, then so is (s)(s)(s).
if sss and ttt are balanced bracket sequences, then so is ststst.
Note:‚àíNote :-Note:‚àí The input files are large. The use of Fast I/O is recommended.

###Input


The first line contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of testcases.
The first line of each test case contains the string SSS.
The next line contains a single integer QQQ denoting the number of cases to consider.
The next line contains QQQ space separated integers, each denoting tit_iti‚Äã.
###Output

For each query, print the minimum value of xxx in a separate line. If no such xxx exists, print ‚àí1-1‚àí1.

###Constraints


1‚â§T‚â§1031 \leq T \leq 10^31‚â§T‚â§103
1‚â§‚à£S‚à£‚â§1071 \leq |S| \leq 10^71‚â§‚à£S‚à£‚â§107
1‚â§Q‚â§1061 \leq Q \leq 10^61‚â§Q‚â§106
1‚â§ti‚â§N1 \leq t_i \leq N1‚â§ti‚Äã‚â§N
Every character of SSS is either ((( or ))).
Sum of ‚à£S‚à£|S|‚à£S‚à£ and QQQ over all testcases for a particular test file does not exceed 10710^7107 and 10610^6106 respectively.

Explanation:
For the first query, Chef chooses to let S1S_1S1‚Äã pass through him, gets hit by S2S_2S2‚Äã and finally completes a balanced bracket sequence by adding S3S_3S3‚Äã to S2S_2S2‚Äã at time xxx = 333.
